title: consolidate `ps` behavior between `podman ps` and `podman pod ps`
body:
`podman ps` applies a default "running" filter and by default prints a list of running containers. This is said to have been done for Docker compat:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/90f38bc91141d4ff8de614e50e627a630c44c0ac/pkg/ps/ps.go#L55-L66

Whereas `podman pod ps` has no such filter and so, by default, it prints a list of pods in any state.

It might be worthwhile to have the same behavior for both. Docker doesn't have pods, so there's no 1-1 compat and perhaps that's the reason for the inconsistency.

Follows from #28707

comments.body:
I think podman ps is one of the most common commands, I rather not change its default behavior.

Switching podman pod ps on the other hand to only show running pods also does not seem to useful for me, it is harder to define what a running pods is, only show pods where all containers are running or where at least one is running?

Unless we get a lot of user complains I see no reason to switch long standing behaviours here.

Fair enough, though the suggestion was to mirror `podman pod ps`'s behavior with that of `podman ps` ie showing running pods.  But you're right, the definition of a running pod is not clear for us, though k8s defines it quite cleanly:

```
Running	The Pod has been bound to a node, and all of the containers have been created. At least one container is still running, or is in the process of starting or restarting.
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
